Article Title: Infiltration bid foiled: Army engages in cross-border firing in J&K's Rajouri after spotting suspicious movement
Article Text: NEW DELHI: The Army engaged in cross-border firing along the Line of Control (LoC) in Jammu and Kashmir's Rajouri district after troops detected suspicious movement near the border, officials said on Saturday.According to the officials, Army personnel opened fire after spotting suspected terrorists near the LoC.The exchange of small arms fire was reported from the Tarkundi forward area around 10 pm on Friday and continued for nearly one-and-a-half hours. There were no reports of any casualties.Officials said the firing by Indian troops was followed by retaliatory small arms fire from across the border.A search operation was later launched to ascertain whether any infiltration had taken place, while the area remains under heightened surveillance, officials added.
Article Summary: The exchange of small arms fire was reported from the tarkundi forward area. There were no reports of any casualties. A search operation was later launched to ascertain whether any infiltration had taken place. The area remains under heightened surveillance, officials added.

Article Title: India to engage with US over immigration & visa issues
Article Text:
Representative image

NEW DELHI: Ministry of external affairs said on Friday that while visa rules and immigration matters are sovereign decisions of any country, India will continue to engage with the US on the difficulties faced by Indian travellers and students seeking to visit the US."When issues concerning genuine travellers seeking to visit the US are brought to our attention, we take them up with the US side to minimise difficulties for our people," MEA spokesperson said.He was responding to queries related to the US's tightening of visa rules for foreign students, exchange visitors and journalists.The new regulations establish a fixed period of admission for non-immigrant visa holders in F, J, and I classifications, Department of Homeland Security said in a statement on Thursday. The F category visas are for students, the J category for exchange visitors, and the I category is for journalists.
Article Summary: India will continue to engage with the us on the difficulties faced by indian travellers and students seeking to visit the us. The us's tightening of visa rules for foreign students, exchange visitors and journalists. The new regulations establish a fixed period of admission for non-immigrant visa holders.

Article Title: ‘Just like 'Gandhi' film not based on Mahatma’: Aamir Khan faces political backlash for remarks on Sonam Wangchuk
Article Text:
Aamir Khan's remarks on Sonam Wangchuk not being inspiration for '3 Idiots' sparks political backlash

Political reaction to comment

NEW DELHI: Aamir Khan ’s controversial remarks in relation to Sonam Wangchuk not being the inspiration behind Phunsukh Wangdu in “3 idiots” has led to a rebuke from political parties who accused the actor of being unwilling to take a stand out of fear of the ruling establishment.Responding to a question at the London Indian Film Festival, Khan said that neither he nor director Rajkumar Hirani or writer Abhijat Joshi knew Wangchuk when they made the film."No, that's not true actually. That is a misconception. I didn't know about Mr Sonam at that time when we were doing this film '3 Idiots'... However, what Mr Sonam is doing is good work in any case. He doesn't have to be based on the character of '3 Idiots' for us to respect him and the work that he does," Khan said.Asked about Wangchuk's hunger strike over alleged irregularities in the NEET examination, Khan said: "I think all of us are very concerned for his health... We hope that it ends well. All of us are hoping he ends his fast and looks after his health."RJD Rajya Sabha MP Manoj Jha questioned the timing of Khan's clarification. "It took them years to say this. I don't even remember how many years ago the film was released. Until now, everyone believed the same thing.Only now have you realised that this is what should have been said. It shows having a spine in front of the state is not an easy option," Jha told PTI.Senior Congress leader Husain Dalwai said he wonders why Khan is "so scared." He said Khan should "learn from actor Naseeruddin Shah on how to speak fearlessly" and at least express sympathy for Wangchuk.AAP Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Singh also criticised the actor. "Whether it is Aamir Khan or anyone else, this Modi government has exposed everyone's character. They have all bowed before it. They do not have the courage to speak," Singh said.Advocate and activist Prashant Bhushan said Khan's response shows that "he is now a pale shadow of the person who made '3 Idiots' and many other revolutionary films."Former Congress leader Sanjay Jha also took a swipe: "Now Aamir Khan says Phunsukh Wangdu in '3 Idiots' is not inspired by Sonam Wangchuk. Correct. Just like 'Gandhi', the 1982 film is not based on Mahatma Gandhi."Sonam Wangchuk has entered the 20th day of his indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar. He has been protesting since June 28, demanding education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over alleged multiple exam irregularities.
Article Summary: Aamir khan's remarks on sonam wangchuk not being inspiration for '3 idiots' sparks political backlash. Khan said neither he nor director rajkumar hirani or writer abhijat joshi knew wangchuk when they made the film. Senior congress leader husain dalwai said he wonders why khan is "so scared"

Article Title: Evening news wrap: PM Modi flags off India's first hydrogen-powered train; Andy Burham set to become next UK PM, and more
Article Text:
Hydrogen-train flagged off while Andy Burnham is set to become UK PM

PM Modi on Friday flagged off India's first hydrogen-powered train.

Andy Burnham is set to be new UK Prime Minister, takes over from Keir Starmer on Monday.

A BJP MLA in MP sparked controversy after refused to hear grievances of villagers from a neighbouring Assembly constituency.

MLA in MP sparked controversy after refused to hear grievances of villagers from a neighbouring Assembly constituency. An Iran-backed militia umbrella announced a $10 million reward for anyone who 'kills" US President Donald Trump.

India's U-19 World Cup-winning star was arrested over alleged attempt to bribe player.

Here are top 5 stories of the day

PM Modi flags off India's first Hydrogen-powered train

Andy Burnham set to be new UK Prime Minister, takes over from Keir Starmer on Monday

'Go to your MLA': BJP legislator's response to water crisis sparks protest

'Whoever kills Trump ...': Iran-backed Iraqi militia puts $10 million bounty on US president

India's U-19 World Cup-winning star arrested over alleged attempt to bribe player

Prime Minister Narendra Modi flagged off India's first hydrogen-powered train. This ushers India into a group of select countries that have hydrogen trains. India’s first hydrogen-powered train has 10 coaches, with the capacity to carry 2,600 passengers. It will run between Jind and Sonipat in Haryana at a maximum operational speed of 75 kmph, though it is designed for 110 kmph. Read full story Andy Burnham has been elected leader of the Labour Party. He is set to become the next prime minister of the United Kingdom on Monday, formally replacing Kier Starmer. Burnham was the only contender in the leadership contest to replace departing Starmer. He secured nominations from 379 of the 403 Labour lawmakers in the House of Commons, surpassing the threshold to proceed to the next stage. Read full story A BJP MLA in Madhya Pradesh's Shivpuri district landed in controversy after he allegedly refused to hear grievances of villagers from a neighbouring Assembly constituency, telling them they were "not his voters". Later, an agitated villager placed his young child on the bonnet of the legislator's parked car in protest.The incident occurred during a Jan Chaupal (public grievance meeting) organised by Karera MLA Ramesh Khatik at Dr Bhimrao Ambedkar Bhawan in Narwar tehsil. Read full story An Iran-backed militia umbrella calling itself the Islamic Resistance in Iraq (IRI) has announced a $10 million reward for anyone who kills US President Donald Trump. In a statement dated July 16, the group said it was offering the reward to "whoever kills the criminal Trump, or whoever eliminates him personally, directs others to do so, or facilitates it as an individual, group, organisation or institution." Read full story Former India Under-19 World Cup winner Manjot Kalra was arrested by Sri Lanka's sports anti-corruption investigators over allegations of attempting to bribe a player during the Lanka Premier League (LPL). Kalra, who struck a match-winning century in the final of India's victorious 2018 ICC Under-19 World Cup campaign, was taken into custody by the Police Special Investigation Unit for the Prevention of Offences Relating to Sports. He was later produced before a magistrate. Read full story
Article Summary: Hydrogen-train flagged off while andy burnham is set to become uk pm. India's u-19 world cup-winning star was arrested over alleged attempt to bribe player. A bjp mla in mp sparked controversy after refused to hear grievances of villagers from a neighbouring assembly constituency.

Article Title: 'No proof, no facts': Former envoy Sanjay Verma on Nijjar case as US indictment clears India
Article Text:
Hardeep Singh Nijjar

US indictment clears India, points to gang war

India had warned Canada

US indictment: A turning point

NEW DELHI: Former Indian Ambassador to Canada Sanjay Verma has defended India's position on the Hardeep Singh Nijjar case, asserting that allegations linking India to the killing were "baseless" and unsupported by evidence. He also maintained that New Delhi has consistently warned Canada about Khalistani extremist activities."Whatever allegations were levelled were grave, but there was no proof or fact behind those allegations. There was nothing that could be called 'credible evidence' in legal terms, and India stood firm on its stance," Verma told ANI.He said India had rejected the allegations from the outset, calling them "politically motivated" and later "completely absurd." "India has remained consistent on that statement from then until today," he added.Verma pointed to recent developments in the United States, where a federal investigation into organised crime networks concluded that Nijjar's killing was the result of a gang war between two factions, with no involvement of the Indian government, officials or diplomats."If we look at recent developments, there was an investigation in the US into what they call an 'organised crime group.'This investigation took place over the last three years, and Canada's investigative agencies were also a part of it. The conclusion reached through their investigation, and this is a US conclusion, is that the assassination was the result of a gang war, a fight between two factions," Verma said.He cited remarks by the Royal Canadian Mounted Police deputy commissioner, who said in a TV interview that no Indian officials, the nation of India, the Indian government, or Indian diplomats were charged or had any hand in the killing.When asked if the allegations caused damage to India's international image, Verma said that the claims made by former PM Justin Trudeau had lost weight over time."Initially, if a leader of a democracy, at that time, the Prime Minister of Canada was the leader of Canada, makes an allegation, most people will think there must be some truth to it. But as the matter progressed and people examined it from various angles, everyone across the world, including the 'Five Eyes,' reached the conclusion that it was an absurd statement. Levelling blame against India based on that statement is complete nonsense," he said.Addressing concerns over Khalistani activities, Verma said India's concerns predated the Nijjar case and dated back to the 1985 Air India Flight 182 bombing."India has consistently and repeatedly told Canada since then that anti-India activities by Khalistanis take place there. India presented many extradition requests to Canada, but not a single one has been successful so far. It's not as if India started talking about Khalistan because of this murder. India has been talking about Khalistan from the beginning," Verma said.On July 7, US federal prosecutors in Los Angeles announced three indictments naming 37 accused linked to transnational organised crime and narcotics networks under "Operation Hard Ball." The joint investigation involved the FBI, LAPD, RCMP and European law-enforcement agencies.The indictment describes Lawrence Bishnoi and the Organised Crime Network as a prison-directed enterprise that combined extortion, contract killing, and narcotics trafficking across Canada, the US and Europe. The document has no mention of the Indian state, marking a significant turnaround from earlier accusations, especially those from Canada.
Article Summary: Former indian ambassador to canada sanjay verma has defended india's position on the hardeep singh nijjar case. Verma said allegations linking india to the killing were "baseless" and unsupported by evidence. He also maintained that new delhi has consistently warned canada about khalistani extremist activities.

Article Title: 'Risking life won't yield results': Pawan Khera as Wangchuk's hunger strike enters Day 20
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Congress leader Pawan Khera on Friday met activist Sonam Wangchuk and other protesters at Jantar Mantar as their indefinite hunger strike entered its 20th day.Speaking to reporters after the meeting, Khera expressed concern over Wangchuk's deteriorating health and said the Centre was insensitive to democratic protests."We are all concerned about Sonam Wangchuk's health. We are dealing with a highly insensitive government that fails to understand the language of democratic protest. When facing such a government, the methods of protest must evolve. Risking one's life against this government will not yield results," he said.Khera said the Congress has been raising issues related to alleged irregularities in the education system through its "Chhatron Ki Goonj" campaign."We have been running a campaign called 'Chhatron Ki Goonj' for quite some time. Our people are actively raising these issues across every street, lane and campus. We are demanding not only the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan but also transparency in examinations," he said.Khera's visit came a day after Congress formally extended support to Wangchuk's agitation.On Thursday, Congress general secretary (organisation) K C Venugopal appealed to Wangchuk to end his fast, saying the party shared his concerns and would continue demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan."His concerns are our concerns," Venugopal said in a post on X, while urging Wangchuk to end his indefinite hunger strike in view of his health.Meanwhile, Wangchuk's weight loss crossed nine kilograms on the 19th day of his fast, with doctors warning that he had entered a critical stage and that the prolonged hunger strike could begin affecting his organs.In a video message released on Wednesday night, Wangchuk ruled out ending his fast despite appeals from political leaders and supporters, saying doing so without any response from the government would send the wrong message.The Cockroach Janta Party has been protesting at Jantar Mantar, demanding Pradhan's resignation over the alleged NEET examination irregularities. The group has also called for a Parliament march on July 20, the opening day of the Monsoon session.
Article Summary: Congress leader pawan khera met sonam wangchuk and other protesters at jantar mantar. He expressed concern over wangchuk's deteriorating health and said the centre was insensitive to democratic protests. The cockroach janta party is demanding the resignation of union education minister dharmendra pradhan.

Article Title: Long-term exposure to air pollution could lead to dementia, warns WHO
Article Text: NEW DELHI: WHO has issued new global guidelines saying up to 45% of dementia cases could be prevented or delayed by tackling risk factors such as high blood pressure, diabetes, smoking, physical inactivity and, for the first time, air pollution, while advising against the routine use of vitamin and omega-3 supplements for dementia prevention in people without a diagnosed deficiency.The updated recommendations, the first major revision since 2019, reflect growing scientific evidence that lifestyle choices, management of chronic diseases and environmental exposures can significantly influence brain health. The inclusion of air pollution marks a key addition to WHO's dementia prevention guidance, reflecting mounting evidence linking long-term exposure to polluted air with cognitive decline.Dementia is a progressive brain disorder that impairs memory, thinking and the ability to perform everyday activities. Alzheimer's disease accounts for an estimated 60-70% of all dementia cases. Although there is no cure, WHO says a substantial proportion of the risk can be reduced through healthier lifestyles and better management of chronic diseases.The recommendations are particularly relevant for India, where the burden of dementia is expected to rise as the population ages.A nationally representative study published in the peer-reviewed journal Alzheimer's & Dementia estimated that about 8.8 million Indians aged 60 years and above are living with dementia, based on a prevalence of 7.4% among older adults.The WHO guidelines on air pollution are supported by a growing body of evidence. The 2024 Lancet Commission on Dementia Prevention, Intervention and Care identified air pollution as one of 14 modifiable risk factors that together could account for up to 45% of dementia cases, while a 2024 study in BMJ Public Health identified both outdoor and indoor air pollution as important dementia risk factors in the Indian population.Globally, more than 57 million people live with dementia and nearly 10 million new cases are diagnosed every year.The guidelines recommend regular physical activity, quitting tobacco, limiting alcohol consumption, adopting a healthy diet, maintaining social engagement and cognitive stimulation, along with effective management of high blood pressure, diabetes and high cholesterol. The use of hearing aids, where clinically indicated, may also help reduce dementia risk."We know more today than ever before about what drives dementia risk, and these guidelines translate that knowledge into action," WHO director general Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said. "Countries now have clear, evidence-based recommendations they can put into practice immediately to protect people's cognitive health."WHO also advises against routine supplementation with vitamins B or E, omega-3 fatty acids or multivitamin-mineral preparations to prevent dementia in people without a diagnosed deficiency, citing insufficient evidence of benefit.Dementia also imposes a heavy economic burden. WHO estimates the disease costs the global economy about $1.3 trillion annually, with around half of that burden borne through unpaid care provided by family members and friends.
Article Summary: Who says up to 45% of dementia cases could be prevented or delayed by tackling risk factors. High blood pressure, diabetes, smoking, physical inactivity and, for the first time, air pollution are included. Who advises against the routine use of vitamin and omega-3 supplements for dementia prevention.

Article Title: Modi speaks to Qatar Amir, recalls legacy of his father
Article Text: NEW DELHI: PM Narendra Modi spoke to Qatar Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani to convey his condolences on the passing of the latter's father Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, the Father Amir of Qatar.Recalling the Father Amir's significant contributions as the chief architect of modern Qatar, PM paid tribute to his visionary leadership and recalled his pivotal role in strengthening India-Qatar relations over the years as well as his deep affection for India and the Indian community in Qatar, according to an Indian readout."Recalled his deep affection for India, his warmth towards the Indian community in Qatar and his steadfast commitment to India-Qatar relations. His enduring legacy will continue to guide our two countries as we further strengthen our strategic partnership," Modi posted on X.India had earlier this week declared a day of national mourning on the passing of the Father Amir, acknowledging his contribution to the bilateral relationship. Qatar remains one of India's most important partners in the Gulf, as it is home to a sizeable Indian diaspora and also a principal source of energy, particularly LNG."The Amir of Qatar thanked the PM for his call and conveyed his appreciation for the words of support in this difficult hour. The two leaders reaffirmed their resolve to carry forward the Father Amir's legacy and further strengthen the India-Qatar strategic partnership and people-to-people ties," said Indian govt, adding that the leaders agreed to remain in close touch.
Article Summary: Pm narendra modi spoke to qatar amir sheikh tamim bin hamad al thani. Pm paid tribute to his visionary leadership and recalled his pivotal role in strengthening india-qatar relations over the years. India had earlier this week declared a day of national mourning on the passing of the father amir.

Article Title: White House sends clear message after calls for FIFA to punish Argentina players over Falklands banner
Article Text:
Argentina players hold a banner with the words "The Malvinas are Argentine", referring to the Falkland Islands, at the end of the World Cup semifinal soccer match between England and Argentina in Atlanta, Wednesday, July 15, 2026.(AP Photo/Rebecca Blackwell)

Sky News question prompts White House response

"Were Argentina's players wrong to use a World Cup stadium to claim sovereignty of the Falkland Islands, which is territory of your great ally, the United Kingdom?"

"We believe in our First Amendment rights here in the United States of America,"

"I think this is going to be an unbelievable final. And in terms of the ability, the opportunity to be able to make those statements, they have the ability to do that in the United States of America."

Why the banner has become controversial

Argentina's Giovani Lo Celso holds a banner with the words "The Malvinas are Argentine", referring to the Falkland Islands, while teammate Argentina's Nicolas Otamendi approaches, at the end of the World Cup semifinal soccer match between England and Argentina in Atlanta, Wednesday, July 15, 2026. (AP Photo/Rebecca Blackwell)

FIFA reviewing the incident

"As is standard procedure, FIFA's independent Disciplinary Committee is currently assessing the match reports and considering the relevant circumstances before deciding on potential further steps based on the FIFA Disciplinary Code."





Original briefing focused on World Cup operations

The White House has publicly defended the right of Argentina's players to display the controversial Falklands banner following their World Cup semi-final victory over England, marking the latest political development in a disciplinary case that FIFA is still reviewing ahead of Sunday's final against Spain.The comments came during an official White House FIFA World Cup Task Force media briefing on Friday, a press conference primarily convened to provide operational updates on the tournament, including security arrangements, preparations for the World Cup final in New Jersey, and concerns surrounding haze caused by Canadian wildfires affecting air quality in the region.However, much of the discussion quickly shifted to the controversy surrounding Argentina's post-match celebrations following their dramatic 2-1 comeback victory over England in Atlanta.During the question-and-answer session, Sky News sports correspondent Rob Harris asked Andrew Giuliani, Executive Director of the White House Task Force for the FIFA World Cup 2026, whether the United States believed Argentina's players were wrong to use a World Cup stadium to display a political message concerning the Falkland Islands.Harris asked:Giuliani, who is overseeing the federal government's coordination of the World Cup alongside agencies including the FBI, Department of Homeland Security and other departments responsible for tournament operations, declined to criticise the players.Instead, he cited the United States Constitution.Giuliani replied.Following the briefing, Harris wrote on X that "the White House backs the Argentina players right to protest about the Falklands at World Cup matches and rejects calls (led by the UK government) to punish them," adding that officials viewed the issue through the lens of freedom of expression rather than sporting discipline.The incident occurred immediately after Argentina defeated England 2-1 to reach a second consecutive World Cup final.Several Argentina players were photographed holding a supporters' banner reading "Las Malvinas son Argentinas" ("The Falkland Islands are Argentine").The Falkland Islands, located around 300 miles east of Argentina and roughly 8,000 miles from the United Kingdom, remain the subject of a long-running sovereignty dispute.Argentina invaded the islands in 1982, prompting a 10-week conflict before British forces retook the territory. The war resulted in the deaths of 255 British service personnel, 649 Argentine military personnel and three Falkland Islanders.The sovereignty dispute remains unresolved and politically sensitive for both countries.Although the White House defended the players' right to express themselves, FIFA's regulations take a different approach.Article 34.3 of the 2026 FIFA World Cup Match Protocol prohibits players and team officials from displaying political, religious or personal messages before, during or after matches.FIFA confirmed earlier this week that its independent disciplinary committee has opened its standard review process.A FIFA spokesperson said:British politicians have urged FIFA to suspend the players involved, while reports from Spanish outlet and Argentine newspaper have suggested that any disciplinary action is more likely to result in warnings or financial penalties than suspensions. Those reports also indicate that any formal decision is expected after the tournament concludes, although FIFA has not confirmed a timeline.Also read: Will Argentina stars miss World Cup final under FIFA rules over Falklands banner celebration The Falklands issue emerged during a briefing that had originally been organised to discuss preparations for Sunday's final between Argentina and Spain.Officials also addressed concerns surrounding smoke drifting from Canadian wildfires, which has affected air quality across parts of the northeastern United States.According to officials, contingency planning remains in place, but there is currently no expectation that the World Cup final will be disrupted. Forecasts for East Rutherford continue to predict conditions well below the threshold that would require postponement or cancellation, allowing organisers to proceed with preparations for football's biggest match.
Article Summary: The white house has publicly defended the right of argentina's players to display the controversial falklands banner following their world cup semi-final victory over england. The incident occurred immediately after argentina defeated england 2-1 to reach a second consecutive world cup final.

Article Title: After Andhra, COVID-19 resurfaces in Maharashtra after 3 years
Article Text:
Officials said most positive cases were detected during physician-advised investigations or pre-operative fitness assessments rather than routine Covid testing. (Representative image)

Revive good practices

PUNE: Covid has resurfaced in Maharashtra and the city recorded a significant increase in case count, mostly mild, over the past 10 days though there is no indication of a fresh wave.The state health department data showed Maharashtra recorded 48 Covid-19 cases so far this year, with no death. Cases gradually increased through 2026, after remaining largely under the radar for nearly three years. Three Covid cases were reported in Jan, one in Feb, 11 in June and 21 in July, so far.Officials said most positive cases were detected during physician-advised investigations or pre-operative fitness assessments rather than routine Covid testing.Experts said the virus had now settled into a pattern similar to influenza. Periodic spikes were expected as immunity waned and new variants emerged, they said.Doctors said the recent uptick was the first noticeable increase in Covid cases since around 2023. Dr Piyush Chaudhary, infectious diseases specialist at Jehangir Hospital, said both Covid and influenza cases increased over the last eight to 10 days."We've seen at least seven to eight Covid cases at our hospital alone during this period.If one centre is getting these numbers, there are likely to be several more cases across the city," he said.Dr Chaudhary said the illness was largely milder than even the Omicron variant. Patients are typically reporting cough, cold, sore throat and fatigue, lasting three to five days.Many of those testing positive were hospitalised for unrelated medical conditions or were screened before procedures rather than because of severe Covid symptoms."The actual number of infections in the community is likely to be several times higher because many people with mild cold and cough never get tested. Covid is here to stay, much like influenza. These viruses mutate over time, leading to fresh spurts every one or two years as population immunity declines. People should not let their guard down,” Dr Chaudhary said.Dr Sujata Rege, consultant in infectious diseases at Symbiosis University Hospital and Jupiter Hospital, said Covid occurred sporadically over the past few years but did not attract much attention because the testing had reduced.She explained that hospitals frequently used lower respiratory multiplex panels, which do not include Covid, while upper respiratory panels do.Respiratory multiplex panels are advanced molecular diagnostic tests, such as multiplex PCR, that simultaneously detect numerous viral and bacterial pathogens from a single patient sample.Dr Rege said, "Previously, we would see only an odd Covid case here and there. Now, with more viral respiratory infections circulating, Covid is showing up again because the upper respiratory viral panels are being used more often."She stressed that the current situation did not resemble a Covid wave."It's another respiratory virus that waxes and wanes. We're still seeing sporadic infections rather than every patient testing positive," she said, adding that hospitals were simultaneously getting increased cases of influenza, dengue, chikungunya and typhoid.Consultant pulmonologist Dr Shraddha Kulkarni of Jupiter Hospital also saw a few Covid-positive patients in the past few days after hardly encountering any for a long time."These patients with underlying respiratory illnesses developed fever and tested positive when we screened them for respiratory viruses. So far, all cases have been mild, but we need to closely monitor the trend over the next few weeks," she said.
Article Summary: Officials said most positive cases were detected during physician-advised investigations or pre-operative fitness assessments. Maharashtra recorded 48 covid-19 cases so far this year, with no death. Cases gradually increased through 2026, after remaining largely under the radar for nearly three years.

Article Title: Burnham is Labour chief, to take over as PM on July 20
Article Text:
Andy Burnham Andy Burnham Andy Burnham 1

2

LONDON: Andy Burnham promised to bring hope to the British people and purpose to the country's floundering Labour govt as he was officially declared party leader on Friday, clearing his final hurdle to take office as prime minister on July 20.The former mayor of Greater Manchester was the only contender in the centre-left party's leadership contest to replace departing PM Keir Starmer. Friday's announcement was a foregone conclusion after Burnham secured nominations from 379 of the 403 Labour lawmakers in the House of Commons."We're going to give them hope back," Burnham, 56, told an audience of lawmakers, party activists and trade union leaders in his first speech as leader. "This is a proud moment you have given me and my family today, and an emotional one, but it is one for which I am ready."He sketched out some priorities in Friday's speech, promising to deliver "hope in every heart" and "good growth in every postcode," in part by transferring power from central govt in London to local leaders in cities and regions. "We will take power back from Westminster and Whitehall and give it to the place you live," he told the audience."More power over life's essentials so you can make them work better."Starmer will remain prime minister until Monday, when he formally tenders his resignation to King Charles III. The king will then ask Burnham to form a govt. Britain's parliamentary democracy allows governing parties to change leaders, and thus prime ministers, without the need for a general election. The next national election doesn't have to be held until 2029. AP
Article Summary: Andy burnham was officially declared party leader on friday. The former mayor of greater manchester was the only contender in the contest. Burnham secured nominations from 379 of the 403 labour lawmakers in the house of commons. He will take office as prime minister on july 20.

Article Title: Nolan Wells update: FBI investigates threats while unanswered final hours keep death case under intense scrutiny
Article Text:
Nolan Wells (Image via Getty)

FBI says it is helping Jackson County Sheriff’s Office while looking into threats tied to Nolan Wells case

Nolan Wells family continues to seek answers as official and independent autopsy reports are still pending

The investigation into the death of 18-year-old student-athlete Nolan Wells has taken another turn, with the FBI confirming it is helping address threats made against people connected to the case. While the Jackson County Sheriff’s Office continues to lead the investigation, federal officials say they are providing support where needed. At the same time, many questions about Wells’ final hours remain unanswered, and both the official and independent autopsy reports have not yet been released.According to WLBT, the FBI’s Jackson Field Office said it has been assisting the Jackson County Sheriff’s Office since the beginning of the investigation. The sheriff’s office remains the lead agency, but the FBI said it has stepped in when federal resources could help.In its statement, the FBI said:“From day one, we have been supporting Jackson County as they’ve worked to gather information and find answers for the Wells Family and the community. The FBI will remain ready to assist JCSO investigators as they request our support, particularly when federal resources may expand their capacity to provide clarity and peace of mind for Nolan’s loved ones.”The agency also confirmed it is investigating “interstate threats” made against individuals connected to the case. Officials did not identify who received the threats or explain their nature.Some people close to Wells have already spoken about receiving online abuse. High school friend Tracestin Shepherd, in an interview with ABC News that aired on Good Morning America, said he and others have faced threats even though they have not been accused of wrongdoing.“We’re all young adults and we’re still learning this whole adulthood slash life thing. We did no wrong here, and we don’t understand how we’re getting so much hate behind it.”According to earlier reports, Ashlee Cole, a local judge whose son was with Wells during the Fourth of July trip, also deleted her social media accounts after receiving online harassment.Nolan Wells disappeared after attending a Fourth of July gathering on Horn Island, a barrier island off the Mississippi coast. He was later found dead, and investigators have said they have not found evidence of foul play. Law enforcement believes drowning is one possible cause of death, but the investigation remains open.Wells’ parents, Christine and Elmore Wonsley, have repeatedly said they are not convinced by the current explanation of what happened. They have questioned how their son could have become separated from his friends, leave his phone behind on the boat and later die without anyone noticing.Their attorney, Ben Crump, met with the Jackson County District Attorney on July 15. After the meeting, Crump said the case will be presented to a grand jury once investigators complete their work.“The hope is that when this is presented to the grand jury, all relevant witnesses and evidence will be presented to them, so that we can have a fair and impartial investigation.”As of now, neither the official autopsy nor the independent autopsy requested by the family has been completed. Those findings are expected to play an important role in understanding what happened during Wells’ final hours.
Article Summary: Fbi says it is helping jackson county sheriff’s office while looking into threats tied to nolan wells case. Officials did not identify who received the threats or explain their nature. High school friend tracestin shepherd, in an interview with abc news, said he and others have faced threats even though they have not been accused of wrongdoing.

Article Title: BTS World Cup Halftime Show: Date, timing, venue and where to watch in the US, Canada, Mexico, India, Japan, and more
Article Text:
BTS is set to headline the historic FIFA World Cup 2026 Final Halftime Show, marking the tournament's first-ever halftime spectacle. The performance will take place during the July 19 final at New York New Jersey Stadium. Fans can watch it through official broadcasters worldwide, including Zee5 in India. The event also features Shakira, Madonna, Justin Bieber and others, while supporting the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund for children's education and sports initiatives.

BTS World Cup Halftime Show : Date, Venue, and Global Timings

first K-pop act to headline a FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show

"a great honour"

"Mmm,"

BTS is preparing for what could be one of the biggest performances of their careers, as the group reunites on football's grandest stage. The septet will serve as headliners of the historic FIFA World Cup 2026 Final Halftime Show, the first halftime entertainment spectacle in a World Cup final. Millions of football fans and ARMY across the world are expected to tune in and viewers are now looking for the exact broadcast timings, streaming platforms and what to expect from the landmark event.The final of the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be played on Sunday 19 July at the New York New Jersey Stadium. Kickoff is set for 3:00 p.m. ET (19:00 GMT).The halftime show is expected to begin around 3:55 p.m. ET, though it could start a little earlier or later depending on stoppage time in the first half and delays to the game. The performance is expected to be about 11 minutes long and FIFA are expected to extend the usual half-time break to allow for stage production and artist performances, reports said.Fans around the world can watch the show through FIFA's official broadcast partners in their respective countries.The 2026 edition introduces a Super Bowl-style halftime show to the FIFA World Cup final for the very first time. FIFA has partnered with Global Citizen for the production, while Coldplay frontman Chris Martin is serving as the creative curator.BTS will share the stage with an impressive lineup that includesThe celebrations will begin even before kick-off. Post Malone is expected to lead the closing ceremony, while Jennifer Hudson is scheduled to perform the national anthem. Several other celebrities, including Tom Cruise, IShowSpeed, Robbie Williams, Nicole Scherzinger and Laura Pausini, are also expected to be part of the World Cup festivities.This performance marks BTS' first appearance together at a FIFA World Cup ceremony since completing military service and resuming full group activities. It also makes them theThe event carries added significance for Jungkook, who previously performedduring the FIFA World Cup Qatar 2022 opening ceremony. His appearance at the 2026 final makes him the first Korean and Asian artist to perform at multiple FIFA World Cup ceremonies.In an official statement, BTS reflected on the opportunity, saying it wasto perform on a stage that brings people together from across the globe. The group added that they believe music can inspire hope and unity, while supporting children's education through the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund makes the occasion even more meaningful.Speculation about new BTS music tied to the tournament continues to grow. During a Weverse Live on July 8, j-hope was asked directly if BTS would release a FIFA song. Instead of confirming or denying the possibility, he simply replied,before shrugging.That brief reaction was enough to send fans into overdrive. While no official announcement has followed, many ARMY members believe the group could still have another surprise planned as the World Cup final approaches.
Article Summary: Bts set to headline the historic fifa world cup 2026 final halftime show. Performance will take place during the july 19 final at new york new jersey stadium. Fans can watch it through official broadcasters worldwide, including zee5 in india. Event also features shakira, madonna, justin bieber and others.

Article Title: Does Lionel Messi have siblings? Everything to know about Messi family behind Argentina football legend
Article Text:
Lionel Messi poses with his brother Rodrigo (L), sister María Sol, father Jorge, mother Celia, nephew Tomás and brother Matías (R) during a private photo session for El Gráfico magazine on October 12, 2003 in Rosario, Argentina. (Photo by Marcelo Boeri/El Grafico/Getty Images)

Does Lionel Messi have siblings? Meet the Messi family

Lionel Messi's immediate family at a glance

Family Member Relationship Current Role Jorge Messi Father Business manager and agent Celia Cuccittini Mother Supports family and charitable activities Rodrigo Messi Older brother Career management and public relations Matías Messi Older brother Leo Messi Foundation operations Maria Sol Messi Younger sister Executive at 525 Rosario

Where did the Messi family grow up?

How Lionel Messi's brothers helped shape his career

Rodrigo Messi manages important business responsibilities

Matías Messi oversees charitable initiatives

Lionel Messi's brothers and their roles

Name Relationship Main Responsibility Rodrigo Messi Older brother Public relations, scheduling, business support Matías Messi Older brother Foundation work, family operations Lionel Messi Younger brother Professional soccer career

Maria Sol Messi built her own professional career

Maria Sol Messi's professional journey

Organization Position Primary Focus The Messi Store Former Brand Manager Fashion branding 525 Rosario Senior Director of Brand Strategy and Creative Content and branding Fashion collaborations Creative partner Lifestyle projects

A quieter public profile

How Jorge Messi and Celia Cuccittini supported Lionel Messi

Celia Cuccittini provided family stability

Lionel Messi's parents

Parent Role Contribution Jorge Messi Father Agent, business manager, early career guidance Celia Cuccittini Mother Family support, charitable work

Lionel Messi's family remains central to his success

Important family milestones

Year Family Milestone Childhood Grew up together in Rosario, Argentina 2000 Lionel moved to Barcelona with support from his family 2017 Family attended Lionel Messi and Antonela Roccuzzo's wedding 2024 Continued expansion of family business projects 2025–2026 Family remained closely involved during Lionel's Inter Miami CF and Argentina commitments

Lionel Messi is generally considered one of the greatest soccer players of all time, but behind his amazing achievements is a family that has been behind him from the very beginning. Before winning trophies like the FIFA World Cup and Copa América, or becoming the face of Inter Miami CF, Messi grew up in Rosario, Argentina, with the help of parents and siblings who played an important role in his development.Messi’s performances on the field have captured the attention of the world, but fans are just as curious about the people closest to him. His father, mother, two older brothers and younger sister all remained important throughout his career. Some have helped run his business interests, others have gone on to carve out their own careers, making the Messi family one of the most recognizable in world soccer.Lionel Messi is the third of four children of Jorge Messi and Celia Cuccittini. He has two older brothers, Rodrigo Messi and Matías Messi and a younger sister, Maria Sol Messi.The siblings grew up in Rosario, Argentina, and shared a love of soccer. Today, all of the members continue to support Lionel while each of them maintains his or her own responsibilities and professional career.The Messi family grew up in Rosario, one of the most important cities for the development of soccer in Argentina. For much of his childhood, Lionel played informal matches with his brothers and cousins before joining local club Newell’s Old Boys.That’s why their humble beginnings and family values remain a big part of Lionel’s public persona today.Lionel Messi shot to global superstardom, but both his older brothers played a quiet, behind-the-scenes role throughout his professional career.He has long been concerned in Lionel’s professional affairs. He has worked alongside his father, Jorge Messi, coordinating scheduling, publicity and a variety of commercial responsibilities for Lionel’s expanding global brand.Lionel has been able to concentrate on Soccer; his work has remained mostly out of the public eye.Over the years, Matías Messi has been engaged in various family business ventures, including the Leo Messi Foundation, which focuses on education and health projects for children.His principal roles have typically been operational, not media appearances.Unlike many celebrity siblings who thrive on public attention, Maria Sol Messi has forged her own path in branding, fashion and media projects.She has previously collaborated with The Messi Store, managing branding initiatives alongside creative professionals. Later, she joined Lionel Messi’s production company, 525 Rosario, working on brand strategy and creative development.She’s into marketing, fashion and entertainment more than pro sports, and that’s what shows up in her work.Maria Sol’s family is one of the most famous among those who play soccer, but she tends to live a somewhat private life. Most public appearances are for family occasions or major career milestones for Lionel, not for personal publicity.Lionel Messi has often spoken about the sacrifices his parents made in his childhood. Jorge Messi was perhaps the most influential in Lionel’s early soccer career. Jorge moved with his son to Spain when Barcelona gave the teenager a chance to attend its famous La Masia academy to help him settle into life abroad.Later, he became Lionel’s business manager and has continued to handle many commercial and contractual matters.Jorge followed Lionel through key stages of his soccer development, while Celia Cuccittini kept the family’s ties to Rosario.She has stayed involved in family activities and charitable causes and has continued to support Lionel in his career.Messi has said that family is more important to him than fame, even as he has become one of soccer’s greatest players.His personal life still revolves around his parents, brothers, sister, wife Antonela Roccuzzo and children. Family members often travel with the players for big events ranging from international tournaments to awards ceremonies and major celebrations.The Messi family has built a vast network of business operations, charitable projects and creative ventures that go beyond the soccer field, in addition to providing emotional support.The story of the Messi family is a reminder that success at the top levels of sport is often built on a solid foundation of support behind the scenes. Whether it’s been through business management, charity, creative leadership or personal encouragement, Rodrigo Messi, Matías Messi, Maria Sol Messi, Jorge Messi and Celia Cuccittini have all had their say.One of the defining aspects of his journey is the family’s close bond, which is still making history with Lionel Messi with Inter Miami CF and the Argentina national team. Their mutual commitment, loyalty and desire to work together have contributed to creating not only one of soccer’s greatest careers, but one of the sport’s most revered family stories.
Article Summary: Lionel messi grew up in rosario, argentina, with the help of parents and siblings. His father, mother, two older brothers and younger sister all remained important throughout his career. Some have helped run his business interests, others have gone on to carve out their own careers.

Article Title: Tommy Paul marries longtime girlfriend Paige Lorenze in beautiful Long Island wedding
Article Text:
Tommy Paul marries longtime girlfriend Paige Lorenze in beautiful Long Island wedding

Vogue

Paige Lorenze and Tommy Paul celebrate their wedding where their story first began

Vogue

Paige Lorenze and Tommy Paul’s engagement led to the celebration they always wanted

Extra Dirty

PEOPLE

Sportskeeda

Paige Lorenze and Tommy Paul are now husband and wife. The couple married on July 14 at Old Westbury Gardens on Long Island, New York, in front of their family and close friends. Paige quietly shared the news by changing her Instagram name to Paige Paul before posting wedding photos with. Their wedding came just over a year after Tommy proposed during a beachside trip in Nantucket in July 2025.The couple wanted the day to reflect their relationship from start to finish. Speaking to, Paige said they chose to marry in the United States because that is where they first met.“I wanted the wedding to feel true to who we are and where our story began.”Paige wore a Carolina Herrera wedding gown because she had always wanted to wear an American fashion house on her wedding day. Tommy chose a custom double-breasted Burberry suit.Paige admitted she expected the walk down the aisle to be the most stressful moment of the day, but it turned out very differently.“I thought those few minutes would be the most nerve-racking of my life, but they weren’t. I felt completely carried by my dad, by our families, and by so much love. There was an incredible sense of peace.”Tommy also shared how emotional he felt while waiting for his bride.“It felt like my heart was going to burst out of my chest, and I couldn’t hold back tears.”After the ceremony, the couple quickly finished their portraits so they could spend more time celebrating with their guests.Paige and Tommy have been together since 2022. They announced their engagement in July 2025 with photos from a beach proposal. Tommy simply wrote, “Forever,” alongside the pictures.Months before the wedding, Paige had already shared what she wanted the celebration to feel like. During an appearance on thepodcast in November 2025, she said she had planned every detail and wanted the wedding to be one big party with people she truly loved.She also toldin December 2025 that she would skip a traditional bridal shower because the wedding celebrations already included several events. Instead, she enjoyed a bachelorette trip with her friends in Nantucket before the big day.Throughout Tommy’s tennis career, Paige has regularly supported him from the stands. In a 2023 interview with, she praised both his success and his sportsmanship, saying she was proud of the way he competed.
Article Summary: Tommy paul marries longtime girlfriend paige lorenze in beautiful long island wedding. The couple married on july 14 at old westbury gardens on long island, new york. Paige wore a carolina herrera wedding gown because she had always wanted to wear an american fashion house on her wedding day.

Article Title: Fact check: Did Lamine Yamal's girlfriend Inés García Santos really dump her alleged long term ex for the star player?
Article Text:
The Spanish superstar Lamine Yamal has been a force to be reckoned with on the field for the 2026 World Cup, spaking chaos among many. As Spain gears up to face Argentina in the finals, all eyes are on Lamine Yamal’s high profile relationship with popular social media influencer Inés García Santos. Amid all the mess, Inés García Santos’ alleged past relationship has sparked chaos among millions.

Did Lamine Yamal’s girlfriend Inés García Santos leave her alleged boyfriend for the Spanish superstar?

Lamine Yamal’s girlfriend Inés García Santos sparks outrage ahead of 2026 World Cup finals



The Spanish superstar Lamine Yamal has been a force to be reckoned with on the field for the 2026 World Cup. As Spain gears up to face Argentina in the finals, all eyes are on Lamine Yamal’s high profile relationship with popular social media influencer Inés García Santos. Amid all the mess, Inés García Santos’ alleged past relationship has sparked chaos among millions.No, there are no confirmations if Lamine Yamal’s girlfriend, Inés García Santos, was indeed in a long term relationship with another man before she met the Spanish superstar.Inés García Santos has strongly denied breaking up with any man before she met the Spanish superstar Lamine Yamal as she said, “I wasn’t going to say anything or give this topic any attention at all. I didn’t want to get involved in this, but at what point has it been said on Twitter (now X) or anywhere else that I had been in a five-year relationship with my friend Gonzalo and that I left him to be with someone else?”For the unversed, ever since Lamine Yamal and Inés García Santos started publicly dating, there have been unverified rumors of a long term relationship she has had with a male friend of hers.A few hours ago, a fan page on X posted, “Imagine you're a well-put-together young man. You have your life in order, soon you're going to marry your girlfriend of 2 years... Then she gets a DM from a famous football player and INSTANTLY leaves you. Hypergamy is brutal” and it has sparked chaos among fans.A fan took to X and posted, “Men literally cheat on their pregnant wives for random women and you want to convince us that you wouldn't leave your gf for a popular actress”so you're saying you wouldn't leave your girlfriend if a famous actress pursued you?”Another fan wrote, “She did him a favor. He dodged a bullet.Nobody wants a woman that goes wherever the wind blows”.A third fan posted, “The guy she commented “I love you baby” is her gay friend, but obviously this won’t stop the incels on this app from showing us their stupidity…”Another fan wrote, “A relationship that ends over one DM probably wasn’t as solid as it looked……..Fame opens doors, but loyalty is a choice.”However, the Spanish superstar Lamine Yamal has kept himself away from all the drama concerning his high profile relationship with Inés García Santos as he eyes a World Cup win for his nation.For now, all eyes remain on Lamine Yamal as he gears up to face Argentina.
Article Summary: The spanish superstar lamine yamal has been a force to be reckoned with on the field for the 2026 world cup. Amid all the mess, inés garcía santos’ alleged past relationship has sparked chaos among millions. There have been unverified rumors of a long term relationship she has had with a male friend of hers.

Article Title: Claude Lemieux son Brendan Lemieux breaks silence after former NHL star incident report reveals new details surrounding tragic death publicly
Article Text:
Stanley Cup Champion Claude Lemieux Dies at 60 (Getty Images)

Claude Lemieux incident report reveals additional information about tragedy

What happened during Claude Lemieux's final hours

Brendan Lemieux urges respect following report

Former National Hockey League forward Claude Lemieux is back in the spotlight after authorities released an incident report that detailed aspects of the events leading up to his death on May 28, 2026. The report has sparked new public debate around the four-time Stanley Cup champion.Brendan Lemieux, the son of Claude Lemieux, was quick to take to social media to respond to the media coverage. He pleaded with the hockey community not to feed speculation about his father’s death, urging compassion and accuracy.The incident report released says Claude Lemieux’s family had grown increasingly concerned about his well-being in the months before his death. Family members believed he may have relapsed after being sober for more than 10 years, the document said.Deborah Lemieux, wife of Claude Lemieux, according to the report, raised those concerns with him the evening of May 27. After their talk, she asked him to get out of the family house.Later that night, Brendan Lemieux used location sharing to find out the whereabouts of his father, at the family’s business property in Lake Park, Florida.Brendan knew Claude Lemieux had a gun in his car, so as a precaution he took it away before going into the warehouse.Brendan Lemieux called emergency services after he found his father inside the building, authorities said. A cellphone and THC edibles were reportedly found at the scene, and toxicology testing was negative for the substances tested. Claude Lemieux’s death was ruled a suicide by officials.After the incident report was widely reported, Brendan Lemieux issued a public statement criticizing inaccurate and speculative coverage. He said some reports included private details that were wrong or fabricated.Brendan Lemieux also credited the hockey community for its emphasis on respect, loyalty and family and asked fans to keep his father in their thoughts without stoking the misinformation circulating on social media.Claude Lemieux had a 21-season National Hockey League career, winning four Stanley Cups with the Montreal Canadiens, New Jersey Devils and Colorado Avalanche. While the latest report has offered a bit of background on his death, Brendan Lemieux’s message has turned the focus to respecting the family’s privacy and honoring what Claude Lemieux did on and off the ice.The release of the incident report has renewed conversations across the hockey world about mental health, addiction recovery and the challenges former professional athletes can face after retirement. While the findings provide additional context surrounding Claude Lemieux's death, Brendan Lemieux's appeal has underscored the importance of relying on verified information and allowing the family space to grieve rather than fueling speculation.
Article Summary: Claude lemieux's death was ruled a suicide on may 28, 2026. Authorities released an incident report that detailed aspects of the events leading up to his death. The report has sparked new public debate around the four-time stanley cup champion.

Article Title: 'They rigged the election, and I got the World Cup': Donald Trump praises Infantino over Balogun decision while reviving 2020 claims
Article Text:
US President Donald Trump and FIFA president Gianni Infantino speak at a reception at Trump Tower in New York, Friday, July 17, 2026. (AP Photo)

US President Donald Trump has said FIFA president Gianni Infantino made the right decision by suspending the red card shown to US striker Folarin Balogun ahead of the United States' Round of 16 match against Belgium at the FIFA World Cup , a game the Americans eventually lost 4-1.Trump made the remarks during a reception at Trump Tower before a FIFA Council meeting on Friday, two days ahead of Sunday's World Cup final.Trump and Infantino walked onto the stage as Lee Greenwood's God Bless the USA played through the speakers. The two stood at the podium for more than two minutes until the song ended before beginning their speeches.Both spoke about the tournament's success, highlighting attendances and television viewership. Infantino thanked Trump for his support in hosting the event.“You put on the greatest human social and cultural event that mankind has ever witnessed,” Infantino said.During his speech, Trump again referred to what he described as the "stolen" 2020 US presidential election, following his televised address in which he made unverified claims that China had interfered in the election.“I said, you know, I was supposed to not be here right now. I was supposed to be president for eight years,” Trump said, referring to the fact that if he had won the 2020 election, he would have left office before the 2026 World Cup.“The polls showed it. Everyone showed it. They rigged the election. And what did I get? I got the World Cup. I got the Olympics. And I brought them here.”Trump then turned his attention to the United States men's national team and Balogun's disciplinary case.“You had unforgettable moments, like probably the most unforgettable is when they gave that gentleman [a red card],” Trump said.“And I was forced to call Gianni and just make a recommendation. I said, Gianni, I’d like to make a recommendation. Let the guy in the game. No, I didn’t say that. I said, I’d like to wage a complaint. And actually, I had no idea what was going to happen.”Balogun's one-match suspension was lifted before the Belgium match, allowing him to play, although the United States were eliminated after a 4-1 defeat.Trump said the outcome removed any debate over whether the suspension had influenced the result.“You know, it’s so much better … because there’s no controversy,” he said.“They won the game, and our team had all of its players. Think of it, if he didn’t allow him, they’d say: ‘We would have won the game if we had our best players.’ So Gianni made yet another of his many good decisions.”Towards the end of the event, Trump said he would like the United States to host another FIFA World Cup in the future.“This time we’ll leave Mexico and Canada out,” he said, drawing laughter from those attending the reception.
Article Summary: Us president donald trump said fifa president gianni infantino made the right decision by suspending the red card shown to us striker folarin balogun. Trump made the remarks during a reception at trump tower before a fifa council meeting on friday. Trump again referred to what he described as the "stolen" 2020 us presidential election.

Article Title: 'Real-life Funsukh Wangdu needs our support’: 3 Idiots actor Omi Vaidya extends support to Sonam Wangchuk amid hunger strike
Article Text:
Actor Omi Vaidya has voiced his support for Sonam Wangchuk

Hunger Strike Day 17





NEW DELHI: Actor Omi Vaidya, who played Chatur Ramalingam in 3 Idiots, has voiced his support for Sonam Wangchuk ’s agitation at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar, urging people to support the education reform activist as his indefinite hunger strike entered its 17th day on Monday.Sharing a video message on social media, Vaidya invoked Wangchuk’s connection with 3 Idiots, saying the man who inspired Aamir Khan’s character Rancho was now fighting a real-life battle."I don't want Funsukh Wangdu to die. Hello idiots, pehchana? This is Omi Vaidya, Chatur from 3 Idiots and Sadiq from The Office, and I have an important message for you. I don't really do this very often, so please listen up," he said.“Many of you know Sonam Wangchuk as the real-life inspiration behind Rancho from 3 Idiots. He has spent years working for education, innovation and the future of young people,” the actor said.Appealing to the public to pay attention to the protest, Vaidya added, “He stood up for students and for change. Today, he needs our support.”"Right now he is on a very long hunger strike. His blood sugar has gone down very much. I don't know if you've heard about this.I don't know if the media is reporting this. But this is a pretty important thing," he added.The actor’s message comes amid growing concern over Wangchuk’s health, with several public figures expressing solidarity with the protesters and calling on the Centre to respond to their demands.Sonam Wangchuk’s health continues to deteriorate.He has lost 8.5 kg, while his blood pressure was recorded at 109/70 mmHg, according to organisers monitoring his condition.Wangchuk joined the Cockroach Janta Party’s protest at Jantar Mantar on June 28 and has been on an indefinite fast since then.The agitation, which entered its 25th day on Monday, seeks Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation over alleged irregularities in national-level examinations.Earlier, a group of eminent personalities, including writer Arundhati Roy, actors Naseeruddin Shah and Ratna Pathak Shah, and economist Jayati Ghosh, urged Wangchuk and other protesters to call off their fast while extending support to their demands.“We worry that their negligence in responding to your demands will only worsen the already fragile state of health that many of you are in,” the signatories said in a joint statement.The statement described the movement as “a marathon, not a sprint” and appealed to the hunger strikers to preserve their strength for the days ahead.The protest organisers have announced a march to Parliament on July 20, coinciding with the start of the monsoon session.
Article Summary: Actor omi vaidya has voiced his support for sonam wangchuk ’s agitation at delhi’s jantar mantar. He urged people to support the education reform activist as his indefinite hunger strike entered its 17th day on monday. Wangchuk is the real-life inspiration behind rancho from 3 idiots.

Article Title: Trump claims Obama, Biden benefited from ‘cover-up’ of China’s 2020 election meddling
Article Text:
The US president also alleged that intelligence officials had hidden information about efforts to prevent his re-election and support Biden's electoral prospects.

US President Donald Trump on Thursday accused officials from the Obama-era intelligence establishment of concealing evidence of alleged Chinese interference aimed at undermining his administration and helping Joe Biden in the 2020 presidential election. He also claimed that documents linked to former President Barack Obama had also been found among material that was supposed to be destroyed.Trump made the allegations during a primetime address, saying recently discovered burn bags contained information provided by “President Barack Hussein Obama” that was meant to be incinerated but was never destroyed. He said the discovery raised serious questions, although he attributed the failure to destroy the material to incompetence rather than deliberate action.“Recently, we found significant numbers of burn bags, information given by President Barack Hussein Obama to be burned. These bags were supposed to be incinerated, but it never happened. We believe this was not done purposely, but rather through incompetence. But the findings are stunning,” Trump said.The US president also alleged that intelligence officials had hidden information about efforts to prevent his re-election and support Biden's electoral prospects.He said raw intelligence obtained by the FBI in 2020, but allegedly buried by officials, indicated that China had attempted to manufacture illegal ballots for Joe Biden.Trump further claimed that CIA and NSA reports on alleged foreign efforts targeting his re-election were deliberately kept out of the Presidential Daily Briefing during his presidency. According to him, one intelligence analyst admitted that the briefing had been “deliberately massaged” to keep information about Chinese activities linked to the election from reaching the president.Another FBI official, Trump claimed, wrote that she was running “a shadow government” to keep intelligence about alleged election interference from becoming public.The allegations formed part of Trump's broader accusation that officials within the intelligence agencies had worked to protect the political interests of those opposed to him while concealing information from both the president and Congress.Trump said CIA reporting from 2018 described efforts to use forces opposed to him to reduce his vote share and prevent his re-election. He also cited a 2019 assessment that alleged China sought to undermine confidence in the US president and influence American business leaders and journalists against him.“The Chinese government wanted the US president to lose the next election,” Trump quoted the intelligence reporting as saying.Trump said the alleged concealment was particularly serious because intelligence agencies had reportedly learned in 2020 that voter registration data involving tens of millions of voters across 18 states had been bought, stolen or hacked by China. He claimed the information was not disclosed to him as president and, “to the best of our knowledge”, was not shared with Congress.Instead, Trump said, officials continued to describe the 2020 election as “the most secure election in the history of our country”.Trump said the documents contained “exact quotes from CIA reporting”, although he added that the identity of the person responsible for one of the quoted assessments remained under review.He has now asked the office of the director of national intelligence, the US department of justice, the FBI and the CIA to investigate how the information was allegedly withheld, fire those involved in the purported cover-up and pursue criminal charges where appropriate.
Article Summary: Us president donald trump accused officials from the obama-era intelligence establishment of concealing evidence of alleged chinese interference. He also claimed that documents linked to former president barack obama had also been found among material that was supposed to be destroyed.
